data.title: Philip Morris Sets New Sustainability Priorities And Smoke Free Value Targets
data.description:
• Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM) has released its Value Report 2025, outlining updated priorities across its smoke free transition.
  • The company has also introduced its Value Plan 2030+, setting new long term focus areas for consumer health impact, climate, biodiversity, and workforce well being.
  • These disclosures expand on prior transformation efforts by linking sustainability goals with business planning and capital allocation over the coming years.

For investors following NYSE:PM, this update sits at the intersection of tobacco, reduced risk products, and sustainability focused business planning. Philip Morris International is known for its cigarette portfolio and its growing range of smoke free products, and the new Value Report 2025 and Value Plan 2030+ indicate how the company is framing that shift internally and for external stakeholders. The emphasis on consumer health impact, climate, biodiversity, and workforce topics helps clarify which non financial factors the company is highlighting alongside its commercial ambitions.

These plans may influence where NYSE:PM directs research and development, acquisitions, and wider capital allocation, particularly as it pursues a smoke free transformation. The new priorities could also shape how the company engages with regulators, supply chain partners, and employees, which are important considerations as investors weigh long term risk and opportunity. For readers, this news provides additional inputs to track alongside traditional financial metrics when assessing the company’s progress.

data.title: Progressive Debt Deals And AM Best Rating Contrast With Share Weakness
data.description:

Progressive, trading at $201.39, comes into this debt issuance with a mixed share price record, including a 24.2% decline over the past year and gains of 51.9% over three years and 133.0% over five years. The recent 5.1% decline year to date and a 5.7% decline over the past month show that the market has been cautious.

The new bond offerings and AM Best’s 'Excellent' ratings provide additional information about Progressive’s balance sheet and funding options, beyond its share price chart. Approach 1: AT&T Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today in dollar terms. AT&T is assessed using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, which focuses on cash available to shareholders after expenses, interest and necessary reinvestment.

The latest twelve month free cash flow sits at about $20.3b. Analyst based projections and subsequent extrapolations point to free cash flow of $18.0b in 2026 and $22.7b by 2030, with further estimates extending out to 2035. These cash flows are discounted back to today to reflect the time value of money and risk, then summed to arrive at an estimated intrinsic value per share.

On this basis, the DCF model suggests an intrinsic value of about $64.72 per share, compared with the recent share price of $28.78. That implies an intrinsic discount of roughly 55.5%, indicating AT&T stock screens as materially undervalued under this approach.

Approach 2: AT&T Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like AT&T, the P/E ratio is a useful way to link what you pay for each share to the earnings that support that share. It helps you see how many dollars the market is currently willing to pay for one dollar of earnings.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how the market views growth potential and risk. Higher expected growth or perceived resilience can justify a higher P/E, while higher risk or weaker sentiment can justify a lower one.

AT&T currently trades on a P/E of 9.18x. This is below the Telecom industry average of about 16.01x and also below the peer group average of 10.86x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for AT&T is 11.73x, which represents the P/E that might be expected given factors such as its earnings profile, industry, profit margin, market cap and risk characteristics.

The Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for AT&T specific factors rather than assuming all telecom stocks deserve similar multiples. data.title: AbbVie Leans Into Consumer Aesthetics With CoolMonth And Neurotoxin Pipeline
data.description:
• AbbVie’s Allergan Aesthetics unit is running its fourth annual CoolMonth promotion in April 2026, offering new patients discounts on CoolSculpting treatments.
  • The company highlighted new aesthetics data at the American Academy of Dermatology meeting, including a next generation neurotoxin candidate.
  • AbbVie reported emerging trends such as GLP-1 users entering the aesthetics segment and patient concerns about cost and unnatural looking results.

For NYSE:ABBV, this CoolMonth campaign underlines how important aesthetics has become alongside its traditional drug portfolio. Allergan Aesthetics is using direct to consumer promotions and education to reach new patients, at a time when interest in non surgical procedures and body contouring is drawing more consumers into clinics. The link to real world trends such as GLP-1 users seeking treatments illustrates how closely this business aligns with broader health and wellness spending.

For you as an investor, the key point is that AbbVie is placing greater emphasis on its consumer facing model, supported by a pipeline asset in neurotoxins and real world data around patient expectations. These moves may affect how the aesthetics franchise contributes within AbbVie’s overall mix over time, and how the company competes on both pricing and perceived quality in a crowded field.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for AbbVie by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on AbbVie.

We've flagged 5 risks for AbbVie. See which could impact your investment.

CoolMonth and the new aesthetics data show AbbVie leaning harder into a consumer oriented growth angle alongside its prescription drug engine. Discounted CoolSculpting offers aim to lower the price barrier for first time patients, while the campaign’s education focus targets people already thinking about body contouring but hesitating on cost or results. At the same time, the TrenibotE neurotoxin data and insights on GLP-1 users looking for aesthetic treatments highlight how Allergan Aesthetics is trying to link science, branding and real world consumer behavior. For you, the takeaway is that AbbVie is treating aesthetics as more than a bolt on, using promotions to widen the funnel and clinical data to support premium positioning in a crowded market that includes players such as Galderma and Revance.

How This Fits Into The AbbVie Narrative

  • The CoolMonth push and neurotoxin pipeline work support the narrative that AbbVie is building multiple growth drivers beyond core immunology and neuroscience.
  • Heavier reliance on discretionary aesthetics demand could challenge the narrative if consumer spending weakens or price sensitivity rises more than expected.
  • The focus on GLP-1 users and aesthetic mindset research is not a large feature of the existing narrative and may represent an extra, consumer led angle that is not fully captured.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for AbbVie to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Aesthetics is a discretionary spend category, so promotions like CoolMonth may need to work harder if consumers pull back or focus strictly on price.
  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged 5 risks for AbbVie, including high debt and a dividend that is not fully covered by earnings, which can limit flexibility if consumer campaigns or new aesthetics assets underperform.
  • 🎁 The CoolMonth campaign could widen Allergan Aesthetics’ patient base and support clinic utilization, especially if new GLP-1 users become repeat customers.
  • 🎁 The TrenibotE neurotoxin data, combined with real world insights into patient concerns about cost and natural results, may help AbbVie differentiate offerings versus competitors over time.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth watching how clinics and consumers respond to the 2026 CoolMonth offers, including uptake by first time CoolSculpting users and the mix of repeat treatments. Pay attention to further updates on TrenibotE, such as longer term safety, duration of effect and how physicians compare it with established toxins from companies like Merz and Ipsen. Also keep an eye on management commentary around aesthetics growth, pricing, and how GLP-1 related demand shows up in Allergan Aesthetics trends across future quarters.

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Akamai Technologies (AKAM) After Its Strong 1 Year Share Price Run?
data.description:
• Investors may be wondering if Akamai Technologies at around US$110 a share still offers value, or if most of the easy gains are already on the table.
  • The stock closed at US$110.22, with a 3.7% decline over the last 7 days, a 12.0% gain over 30 days, 29.5% year to date, 36.9% over 1 year, 41.5% over 3 years, and 6.1% over 5 years. This gives you a wide range of recent return experiences to weigh up.
  • Recent coverage has focused on Akamai's role as a key infrastructure player for content delivery, cloud security, and edge computing. This has often put a spotlight on how stable its cash flows and demand profile might be. That backdrop helps explain why the share price has seen both near term pullbacks and longer term gains as investors reassess risk and potential.
  • Akamai scores a 2 out of 6 valuation checks, so the rest of this article will compare what different valuation approaches say about that price, and then finish by looking at a deeper way to think about what the stock might really be worth.

Akamai Technologies scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Akamai Technologies Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today using a required rate of return, to arrive at an estimate of what the business might be worth per share right now.

For Akamai Technologies, the model used here is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $636.4 million. Analysts provide explicit Free Cash Flow estimates out to 2027, and Simply Wall St then extends those projections further, with the 2030 Free Cash Flow estimate sitting at $1,295.4 million.

Each of these future cash flows is discounted back to today and summed to produce an estimated intrinsic value of $123.40 per share. Compared with the recent share price of about $110, this implies a 10.7% discount, which indicates that the stock screens as slightly undervalued on this model.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Akamai Technologies is undervalued by 10.7%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 62 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Akamai Technologies.

Approach 2: Akamai Technologies Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Akamai Technologies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of current earnings. Investors usually accept a higher P/E when they expect stronger growth or see the business as lower risk, and a lower P/E when growth expectations are more modest or risks appear higher.

Akamai currently trades on a P/E of 35.33x. That sits above the broader IT industry average P/E of 19.34x, but below the peer group average of 39.44x. On their own, those comparisons tell you how the market is pricing Akamai relative to other companies, but they do not adjust for Akamai’s specific earnings growth profile, margins, size, or risk.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio tries to solve that by estimating what a more tailored P/E might be, given factors such as the company’s earnings growth, profit margin, industry, market cap and risk profile. For Akamai, this Fair Ratio is 31.07x, which is below the current P/E of 35.33x. On this measure, the shares look somewhat expensive compared with what the model suggests as a more neutral P/E.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 20 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Akamai Technologies Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to think about valuation, and on Simply Wall St that takes the form of Narratives, where you pick a clear story for Akamai Technologies and tie it directly to assumptions about future revenue, earnings, margins and a Fair Value that you can compare with the current share price.

A Narrative is essentially your investment storyline written into numbers, so instead of only looking at a P/E of 35.33x or a DCF output, you choose whether you see Akamai as closer to the cautious view around a US$66 Fair Value or the optimistic view around US$131.64, each with its own set of revenue, earnings and margin paths.

On the Simply Wall St Community page, these Narratives sit side by side as easy to use templates. When new information such as earnings, guidance or news is released, the underlying forecasts and Fair Values update. This helps you decide if the gap between your chosen Fair Value and today’s price suggests that Akamai is closer to being fully valued or offers room based on the story you believe.

For Akamai Technologies, here are previews of two leading Akamai Technologies Narratives:

🐂 Akamai Technologies Bull Case

Fair value in this bullish narrative: US$131.64 per share.

Implied discount to this fair value at the recent US$110.22 share price: about 16.3%.

Revenue growth assumption used in this narrative: 8.24% a year.

  • Assumes Akamai grows revenue by 8.2% a year over the next 3 years, with profit margins rising from 10.4% to 18.1% as higher margin security and edge products scale.
  • Seats the story firmly in faster adoption of AI, edge computing and security, as well as industry consolidation that could support pricing and longer term contracts.
  • Describes a 2028 outcome where earnings reach US$934.9m and the shares trade on a 23.5x P/E, with a fair value estimate of US$131.64 based on those assumptions and a 10.3% discount rate.

🐻 Akamai Technologies Bear Case

Fair value in this more cautious narrative: US$102.72 per share.

Implied premium to this fair value at the recent US$110.22 share price: about 7.3%.

Revenue growth assumption used in this narrative: 7.58% a year.

  • Assumes revenue grows at 6.1% to around US$4.9b by 2028, with margins improving but not as strongly, leading to earnings of US$765.1m and a 20.7x P/E on those earnings.
  • Highlights that core content delivery is mature and facing commoditisation, while investments in compute and AI infrastructure could weigh on margins if ramp up is slower than planned.
  • Frames the consensus fair value at US$102.72 as reflecting both growth opportunities in security and compute and risks from higher capital needs, customer concentration and strong hyperscaler competition.

Both Narratives use the same recent share price as a starting point, but they lean on different views about how fast Akamai can grow, how far margins can move, and what P/E investors might accept a few years from now. The key step is deciding which set of assumptions feels more realistic and whether your own view sits closer to the bullish or the more measured case.

data.title: Guangshen Railway (SEHK:525) One Off Gain Clouds 34.5% Trailing Earnings Growth Narrative
data.description:
Guangshen Railway (SEHK:525) has just posted its FY 2025 numbers, with fourth quarter revenue at roughly C¥7.5b and a small net loss of C¥30.5m. This translates into basic EPS of C¥0.00 for the period, while trailing twelve month EPS sits at C¥0.20 on net income of C¥1.43b from revenue of about C¥28.7b. Over recent quarters, revenue has moved from C¥7.06b in Q4 2024 to C¥7.45b in Q4 2025. Quarterly basic EPS has ranged from a loss of C¥0.02 in Q4 2024 to C¥0.09 in Q2 2025, alongside a trailing net profit margin of 5% compared with 3.9% a year earlier. For investors, that mix of higher trailing profitability, a one off C¥366.4m gain and a current margin profile sets the stage for a closer look at how durable the earnings power really is.

See our full analysis for Guangshen Railway.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to set these results against the main Guangshen Railway narratives to see which views are reinforced and which might need a rethink.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Revenue Creeps Toward ¥7.5b While Margins Sit At 5%

  • On a trailing basis Guangshen Railway generated about ¥28.7b in revenue with net income of roughly ¥1.43b, which equates to a 5% net profit margin compared with 3.9% a year earlier.
  • What stands out for bullish investors is that trailing earnings grew 34.5% over the past year, yet this sits below the 5 year average growth rate of 51.3%, which can be read as solid growth but not as strong as the earlier trend.
    • Revenue is forecast to grow about 2.9% a year and earnings about 1.3% a year, both below the Hong Kong market forecasts of 8.3% and 12.2%, so growth oriented bulls need to weigh these slower projections against the recent 34.5% earnings rise.
    • The move in net margin from 3.9% to 5% supports the idea of improved profitability, yet the more modest forward growth forecasts suggest bullish expectations may rely more on earnings stability than on rapid expansion.

One Off ¥366.4m Gain Shapes The 34.5% Earnings Jump

  • The trailing 12 month net income of about ¥1.43b includes a non recurring gain of ¥366.4m, so roughly a quarter of the reported profit is tied to a single item rather than recurring operations.
  • Critics highlight that this one off gain complicates the picture for cautious or bearish views, because the 34.5% trailing earnings growth and 5% margin may not fully reflect the underlying run rate of the business.
    • If investors remove the ¥366.4m gain from the trailing figure, the remaining earnings base would be meaningfully lower than ¥1.43b, which weakens the argument that the latest profit level can be taken at face value.
    • The description of an unstable dividend track record alongside this large one off gain gives bears a concrete footing to question how repeatable the current earnings and cash returns really are.

P/E Of 9.9x And Price Below DCF Fair Value

  • The shares trade on a P/E of 9.9x versus about 13.3x for the wider Asian Transportation industry and 15.7x for peers, while the current share price of HK$2.25 sits below a stated DCF fair value of roughly HK$2.52.
  • What is interesting for valuation focused investors is the contrast between this cheaper P/E and the risks around earnings quality, as the stock also sits about 10.8% below the DCF fair value despite the presence of the ¥366.4m one off gain in recent results.
    • The lower P/E relative to industry and peers points to a market discount, yet the inclusion of a sizeable non recurring gain and an unstable dividend history gives a clear reason for some investors to question how much weight to put on trailing earnings multiples.
    • At the same time, earnings growth of 34.5% over the past year and a 5% net margin provide support for investors who see the current valuation gap to the HK$2.52 DCF fair value as reflecting caution rather than clear fundamental weakness.
